content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

June 18, 1998


Renee Whitson
Superintendent
Exeter Union/Exeter Union High School Districts
134 South 'E' Street
Exeter, California 93221

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-98-148

Dear Ms. Whitson:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTIONS

1. Does the Act require you to disclose your interest in a vacation condominium as an "interest in real property"?

2. Do you have a financial interest in one of the couples who co-owns the condominium with you and your husband?

CONCLUSIONS

1. No, you are not required to disclose your interest in the condominium as an "interest in real property" for purposes of the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act.

2. No, you do not have a financial interest in the other owners of the condominium.


¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 - 18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. A-98-148
Page 2

FACTS

Since July 1, 1995, you have been the superintendent for the Exeter Union and the Exeter
Union High School Districts, which are located in Tulare County. You and your husband own a
vacation condominium near Shaver Lake in Fresno County with two other couples. The
condominium is used solely for personal purposes. You do not derive income from renting or
leasing the condominium. You and the other owners schedule the days you will be using the
condominium. When the condominium is not being used by any of the owners, it remains
vacant. Everyone pays an equal portion (33.3%) of the monthly payment and the association and
maintenance fees. Such payments are made to one account from which one payment is made.
You do not receive any payments from any of the other owners, nor can anyone buy time
from another owner.

One of the couples owns an insurance company, which has served, for approximately ten
years, as the school district's broker for medical insurance. The school district is in the process
of bidding for medical insurance. All three employee units of the school district have contractual
rights to negotiate a change in the district's insurance carrier. Although the contract for medical
insurance is with a representative company, i.e., Blue Shield or Key Health, the insurance
provider contracts with the local insurance company to broker and serve the district's account.

ANALYSIS

Conflict of Interest Law, Disqualification

Section 87100 of the Act prohibits a public official from making, participating in making,
or otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the
official has a financial interest. As a superintendent of a school district, you are a public official
under the Act. (Section 82048.)

An official has a financial interest in a decision, within the meaning of section 87100, if it
is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable
from its effect on the public generally, on the official, or on any one of the following:

"(a) Any business entity in which the public official has a direct
or indirect investment worth one thousand dollars ($1,000) or
more,
(b) Any real property in which the public official has a direct or
indirect interest worth $1,000 or more.
(c) Any source of income ... aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or more in value provided to, received by or
promised to the public official within 12 months prior to the time
the decision is made.

File No. A-98-148
Page 3

(d) Any business entity in which the public official is a director,
officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of
management."

* * * *

(Section 87103(a)-(d).)

You would like to know whether you have a potentially disqualifying economic interest
in one of the couples who co-owns a vacation condominium with you and your husband. For
purposes of section 87103(c), the term "income" means any payment received, unless an
enumerated exception applies, and includes any salary, wage, advance, dividend, interest, rent,
proceeds from any sale, gift, loan, or reimbursement for expenses. (Section 82030.) We have
advised that the sharing of ownership does not cause one owner to become a source of income to
the other, unless there are specific facts indicating some other financial relationship exists. (Hart
Advice Letter, No. A-95-131, copy enclosed.)

Your request for advice indicates that every couple pays an equal portion (33.3%) of the
monthly payment and the association and maintenance fees. Such payments are made to one
account from which one payment is made. You do not receive any payments from the other
owners, nor can any couple can buy time from another couple. Under these facts, the other
condominium owners do not appear to be sources of income to you.

For purposes of section 87103(a) and (d), the term "business entity" refers to any
organization or enterprise operated for profit, including a proprietorship, partnership, firm,
business trust, joint venture, syndicate, corporation or association. (Section 82005.) We have
advised that when two or more individuals own a piece of real property for the purpose of
making a profit, a "business entity" is created even though no formal partnership is created.
(Petris Advice Letter, No. A-90-076.) However, the advice in the Petris letter would not apply
to your facts since you and your husband and the other couples use the condominium as a
personal residence and do not presently derive income from it. (Hart Advice Letter, supra.)
Thus, you do not have a disqualifying economic interest in the partnership under section
87103(a) and (d).

In a formal opinion, In re Nord (1983) 8 FPPC Ops. 6, the Commission held that a
limited partner had a potentially disqualifying economic interest in the general partner of a
limited partnership. The Commission reasoned that when a limited partner invests money in a
limited partnership, the limited partner is actually investing in the entrepreneurial skills of the
general partner who ordinarily has the sole discretion and authority to manage the investment. In
a footnote of that opinion, the Commission noted that its determination would also apply in the
context of a regular partnership with two general partners. (In re Nord, supra, fn. 16.) However,
the Nord opinion is not applicable to regular partnerships that have more than two partners where
no single partner holds a controlling position or acts as a managing partner. (Hahn Advice
Letter, No. I-91-311.)

File No. A-98-148
Page 4

We find that you do not have a disqualifying economic interest in the other owners of the
condominium under the Nord opinion because, as discussed above, your arrangement with the
other owners is not an investment in a business entity.

Disclosure Requirements

Pursuant to the Act, the school district has promulgated a conflict of interest code.
(Section 87200-87210.) The school district's conflict of interest code requires you to disclose
specified economic interests that may foreseeably be materially affected by decisions that you
make as a superintendent including your investments, business positions, interests in real
property and income.

You would like to know whether you are required to report your interest in the vacation
condominium as an interest in real property. The term "interest in real property" includes any
leasehold, beneficial or ownership interest or an option to acquire such an interest in real
property located in the jurisdiction. (Section 82033.) Real property is "located in the
jurisdiction" if it is inside the jurisdiction or within two miles of the boundaries of the
jurisdiction. You stated that the school district is within Tulare County, while the condominium
is in Fresno County, near Shaver Lake. Shaver Lake is not within two miles of Tulare County.
Accordingly, you are not required to disclose your interest in the vacation condominium as an
interest in real property.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Julia Butcher
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:JB:tls

Enclosure

I:\Advice Lrs\98148.wpd

Disclosure Requirements
Pursuant to the Act, the school district has promulgated a conflict of interest code.

Accordingly, you are not required to disclose your interest in the vacation condominium as an

5660.
